era_context: Early Bronze Age in the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East. Egypt's Early Dynastic Period (c. 3100–2686 BCE) is in its early phase or about to begin, depending on chronological system. Mesopotamia is in the Early Dynastic period. The Indus Valley Civilisation is developing. Writing systems are recent innovations or still emerging.
disconfirming_evidence: No events identified for this year. The archaeological record for 3149 BCE is extremely sparse and undated to single-year precision. Chronological systems for this period are debated; some scholars place the beginning of Egypt's unified Early Dynastic Period at c. 3100 BCE, others at c. 3000 BCE or later.
historiographic_note: This year falls in the same poorly documented period as 3145–3148 BCE. No contemporary written records survive that can be confidently assigned to 3149 BCE. The historical record is reconstructed from archaeological evidence, radiocarbon dating, and later king lists, none of which permit year-by-year precision. Chronological uncertainty is particularly acute at the boundary between the Predynastic and Early Dynastic periods in Egypt; different scholarly systems place this transition at different absolute dates, creating ambiguity about which years fall into which era.
